behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=Unit. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly includes cross-module gating: "before any downstream processing" (must prove validator result prevents insurance/triage/scheduling calls). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no real AI-output correctness required; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake orchestrator with spy/fake downstream services. Submit input missing multiple required fields; assert one aggregated missing-fields error and assert zero downstream invocations.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Top=Unit, runner-up=Integration. Rejected Integration because failure boundary is pure validation logic with no module handoff in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB validator: valid date, future date, >150 years, malformed strings; assert correct rejection reason per case.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=System. Rejected System because behavior is payload mapping + response interpretation, which is reproducible with local stubbed payer adapter; text does not require real network faults. Integration brake: no required real OS/network behavior beyond module wiring; no AI dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub payer endpoint/client fixture capturing request payload and returning each status variant. Assert outbound patient ID/plan code and resulting internal eligibility state mapping.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Top=System, runner-up=Integration. Rejected Integration because quoted failure mode is real infra: "payer API is unreachable"; real timeout/connection-refused behavior can differ from mocked exceptions and hide failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run against real HTTP client behavior with unreachable payer target (e.g., refused port or forced timeout). Assert eligibility becomes pending and later intake stages still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=Agentic. Rejected Agentic because behavior checks request construction only ("model input payload"), not model judgment quality. Integration brake: no real OS/network requirement if captured via local model stub; no real AI-output dependency; no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms + rubric config using a stub model transport that records request body; assert payload contains both fields exactly.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Top=Agentic, runner-up=Workflow. Rejected Workflow because this is one model decision point, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom fixtures; assert urgency is high at acceptable pass-rate threshold to account for model variability.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=Unit. Rejected Unit because behavior requires persistence handoff: quoted word "stores" implies parser-to-storage boundary, not just local parse logic. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no dependency on real model quality if fixture response used; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response fixture containing score+explanation through parse-and-persist path; assert stored record includes non-empty clinician-readable explanation field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Top=Agentic, runner-up=Integration. Rejected Integration because correctness depends on content quality from the live model (case-specific reasoning vs generic text), which stubs would always hide.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on symptom-specific cases and evaluate explanation text for mention of case symptoms plus explicit urgency rationale; fail generic/template-only outputs.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high=same-day, medium=within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=Unit. Rejected Unit because "first available slot" depends on interaction with calendar state, not only pure function logic. Integration brake: no real external OS/network/subprocess required with local shared DB; no AI-output dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with occupied/free slots across time windows, run scheduler for high/medium urgency, assert selected slot is earliest valid one per SLA.

behaviors.description: Scheduling never double-books when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=System. Rejected System because failure can be reproduced with in-process concurrent workers against the real local shared calendar DB (transaction/locking semantics) without external network/OS dependencies. Integration brake: no AI dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling attempts targeting same candidate slot against local DB with real transaction constraints; assert only one booking succeeds and no duplicate row/slot assignment exists.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Top=Unit, runner-up=Integration. Rejected Integration because behavior is deterministic message composition/templating requirements and does not itself require multi-module handoff evidence.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test confirmation formatter with representative visit types; assert required fields are present and prep instructions match visit type mapping.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Top=Integration, runner-up=Workflow. Rejected Workflow by explicit text: "deterministic stubbed triage output" disqualifies real agentic decision testing. Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess if dependencies stubbed/local; no real AI-output dependency; no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end orchestration in-process with deterministic triage stub (and controlled insurance/calendar fixtures); assert stage order, state handoff, and successful completion artifact.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Top=Agentic, runner-up=Workflow. Rejected Workflow because behavior describes one AI decision stage in this pipeline (live symptom triage) rather than multiple agentic decisions sequenced; quoted pipeline includes mostly deterministic stages plus one live model call.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like live submission through validate→insurance API→live triage→schedule→confirm. Assert autonomous completion and valid final artifacts (eligibility state, urgency, booking, confirmation) without operator steps.
